channel.title: Compassion Benchmark — Daily Research
channel.description: Daily evidence-linked findings on how institutions recognize, respond to, and reduce suffering — scored across 1,155 entities.

item.title: Jun 2: Germany Sub-Threshold: EU Return-Hubs Deal and Court-Ruled-Unlawful Border-Rejection Policy — New RETURN-HUBS-AND-NON-REFOULEMENT Ruling
item.description: No scored movements this cycle. The benchmark's intelligence value tonight is concentrated in sub-threshold evidence patterns that are pre-positioned as forward scoring triggers. Germany produces the cycle's most consequential new signal: the June 1 EU return-hubs framework, Afghanistan/Syria crimin…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: Jun 1: India 22.7 → 15.6 (Delta −7.1): BAND CROSSING Developing → Critical — Government-Directed Refoulement-and-Expulsion Regime
item.description: A single scored movement defines tonight's cycle: India's composite falls from 22.7 to 15.6 (delta −7.1), crossing from Developing into Critical — the benchmark's first Critical-band placement for a large-democracy index member. The ruling is the second application of ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEI…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 31: Burkina Faso 12.5 → 6.3 (Delta -6.2): Active-State-Perpetration Reweight — HRW Crimes Against Humanity Finding Enters Baseline
item.description: A single scored movement defines tonight's cycle: Burkina Faso's composite falls from 12.5 to 6.3 (delta -6.2) after Human Rights Watch's April 2, 2026 report — documenting government and allied militia crimes against humanity, ethnic cleansing of Fulani civilians, and Bayraktar TB2 drone strikes on…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 30: Hungary 50.0 → 50.2 (Delta +0.2): Sulyok Boundary-Watch Resolved — Constitutional Amendment Branch Fires
item.description: A cycle defined by trigger resolution and methodological precision rather than large score movements. Hungary's three-cycle Sulyok boundary-watch closes on the pre-committed 'constitutional amendment' branch: Magyar's Tisza Party will use its two-thirds majority to pursue presidential removal via a…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 29: Turkey 15.1 → 10.3 (Formula Baseline 13.4 → 10.3, Delta -3.1): CHP HQ Stormed, Opposition Leader's Mandate Nullified
item.description: A cycle split between framing corrections and methodology promotions. Turkey's -3.1 downgrade is computed from the formula-correct baseline of 13.4 (stored 15.1 carries a 1.7pt discrepancy); the May 24 riot-police raid on CHP headquarters and the concurrent judicial nullification of elected CHP lead…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 28: Bolivia 30.9 → 28.4 (Delta -2.5): Military Deployment Law Enacted — ACC Dimension Enters Critical
item.description: Bolivia's -2.5 downgrade (30.9 to 28.4) is the only composite movement tonight, but the cycle's analytical weight rests on three methodology outputs spanning the three floor-adjacent entities: Israel, Russia, and Iran. Israel's AUTHORIZED-RESUMPTION-WITH-SYSTEMATIC-DENIAL category captures a pattern…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 27: Hungary 47.7 → 50.0 (Delta +2.3): EU Funds Accord Fires Forward Trigger — Reform Commitment Now EC-Enforceable
item.description: Three formal applies tonight, each with a structurally distinct mechanism. Hungary (+2.3) fires a pre-committed forward trigger after the Magyar-von der Leyen political accord on unlocking €10.4B in frozen EU funds was signed ahead of the anticipated May 29 window; the EU reform commitment now carri…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 26: Elevance Health 37.5 → 30.0 (Delta -7.5): Two Concurrent DOJ False Claims Actions Anchor First Baseline
item.description: Two formal score changes tonight, both first-baseline replacements that displace unanchored placeholder scores with evidence-grounded positions. Elevance Health's -7.5 reflects two concurrent active DOJ False Claims Act enforcement actions — a Medicare Advantage 'cash cow' generating $100M+/year via…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 25: Slovakia 33.6 → 31.6 (Δ -2.0): European Parliament 418-207 Votes to Urge EU Conditionality Mechanism — New Tier-1.5 Methodology Category
item.description: One formal score change tonight: Slovakia moves from 33.6 to 31.6 (Δ -2.0, Developing band sustained) following the European Parliament's 418-207 vote on May 20, 2026, formally urging the European Commission to trigger the EU Rule of Law Conditionality Mechanism — the same instrument that froze €10.…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 24: Turkey 17.6 → 15.1 (Δ -2.5): Riot Police Physically Seize CHP Headquarters — Second-Event-at-Floor Apply
item.description: Two formal score applies in a normal-nightly cycle following yesterday's high-apply event. Turkey moves from 17.6 to 15.1 (Δ -2.5, remaining Critical) after Turkish riot police stormed and physically occupied CHP party headquarters on May 24 — an event qualitatively distinct from the May 21 judicial…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 23: BAND CROSSING — Turkey 22.5 → 17.6 (Δ -4.9): Judicial Removal of Opposition Party Leadership Triggers Critical Reclassification
item.description: Two band crossings and four formal score applies in a single normal-nightly cycle — the highest formal-apply count since the May 21 WIDE event. Turkey's downgrade (22.5 to 17.6, Developing to Critical) is triggered by the Turkish appeals court's annulment of the CHP party congress on May 21, the jud…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 22: Slovakia -5.5: Second EU 'Dismantler' Downgrade in Two Nights — Magnitude Scales with Starting Position
item.description: Normal nightly cycle following the May 21 WIDE event. Slovakia's Liberties.eu 'Dismantler' classification — applied to Croatia at -7.8 one night earlier — now produces a -5.5 formal downgrade for Slovakia, establishing that Dismantler magnitude scales with starting position rather than applying unif…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 21: Hungary +6.3: The Benchmark's First Formal Upgrade — Structural Governance Reversal Scores as Compassion Capacity
item.description: May 21 is the benchmark's widest single cycle: 49 entities assessed, 4 formal score changes applied, 2 upward band crossings applied, 21 first-baselines established, and the symmetric-evaluation rule — that positive evidence is weighted equally with negative — validated for the first time at the for…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 20: Anthropic at Exact 60.0 Boundary: Band-Crossing Proposed Upward, Pending DC Circuit Ruling
item.description: May 20 is the highest event-density night of the current cycle: three forward signals from May 19 all resolved. Meta executed its 7,900-person layoff — and the benchmark discovered surveillance software had been deployed on employee laptops during the pre-notice period. An Amazon warehouse worker di…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 19: Palestine Floor-Designation Evidence Accumulating Across a Band Boundary
item.description: May 19 is a pre-event baseline cycle: 17 entities confirmed, 0 formal score changes, 8 sub-threshold movements documented. The most consequential finding is editorial: Palestine sits at composite 25.0 (Developing), but the accumulated evidence — OHCHR-documented ban of 37 aid groups, ongoing killing…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 18: China Crosses Critical Boundary: Band Crossing Developing → Critical (20.3 → 19.5)
item.description: May 18 is the cycle's first band-crossing night since May 15. The China assessment is the critical decision: a -0.8 delta that crosses from Developing into Critical (20.3 → 19.5) via a quarter-step BND dock for state-facilitation-of-allied-war-crimes-via-dual-use-supply — a methodology category not…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 17: Israel IDP Camp Strike Pattern: 96 Killed Overnight, Parents and Children Together
item.description: May 17 is a sub-threshold cycle: no formal score changes cross the 5-point application threshold, but the day is analytically dense. The headline finding is the India-Pakistan Indus Waters Treaty bifurcation — a single international ruling (PCA Supplemental Award, May 15) scores opposite for two adj…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 16: Oracle First-Baseline: 6 a.m. Email Termination of 30,000 Workers — Boundary at 20.6
item.description: May 16 is a backfill cycle covering a single calendar day. The most consequential finding is Oracle's first-baseline assessment at 20.6 — proposed -7.8 from the published 28.4, landing 0.6pt above the Critical/Developing boundary. The 6 a.m. email termination of up to 30,000 workers (concurrent with…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 15: Anthropic Boundary-Down: Safety Pledge Reversal Under Government Pressure — First Canonical Instance
item.description: May 15 was the methodology's most consequential single-night hold-release cycle: three major entities (Anthropic, Microsoft, Google/Alphabet) came off hold simultaneously, generating five score changes, ten new conduct categories, and four boundary-watch flags. The defining event is Anthropic's RSP…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 14: xAI Third Governance Failure Category — Same-Day Event
item.description: May 14 produced the richest single-night methodology harvest in the benchmark's history: nine new conduct categories documented across fourteen assessments, including the first 'medical-science-denial enforcement' designation (Senegal charging patients with undetectable viral loads for 'voluntary HI…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 13: Hungary MAGYAR-ERA FIRST OPERATIONAL BASELINE — BAND CROSSING: Developing to Functional (37.5 → 41.4). Cabinet decentralized to 16 ministries with 3 new standalone portfolios (health, environment, education); comprehensive audit of ministries and state-owned companies ordered; payment suspension beyond normal operational management enacted; first cabinet meeting held May 13 with drought-response expert consultation and emergency measures. ICC arrest-warrant enforcement reversal credited on INT dimension. Conservative-rounding discount applied from full math reconstruction (43.0) to boundary-case-respecting 41.4. 30-day re-assessment June 9.
item.description: Hungary MAGYAR-ERA FIRST OPERATIONAL BASELINE — BAND CROSSING: Developing to Functional (37.5 → 41.4). Cabinet decentralized to 16 ministries with 3 new standalone portfolios (health, environment, education); comprehensive audit of ministries and state-owned companies ordered; payment suspension bey…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 12: Pakistan BAND CROSSING — Developing to Critical. HRCP 2025 annual report: 273+ enforced disappearances; 531,700 Afghans coerced out; Ahmadi minority killings; 6,500+ GBV cases; 145/148 Gender Gap. Conservative proposed composite 17.5 (precise math 17.2). Delta sub-threshold but band crossings are always material.
item.description: Pakistan BAND CROSSING — Developing to Critical. HRCP 2025 annual report: 273+ enforced disappearances; 531,700 Afghans coerced out; Ahmadi minority killings; 6,500+ GBV cases; 145/148 Gender Gap. Conservative proposed composite 17.5 (precise math 17.2). Delta sub-threshold but band crossings are al…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 11: 72-hour ceasefire methodology held cleanly — Russia's 6-event breach record complete with new 'strategic-format-exploitation' conduct category. New Day-3 Sustained-Conduct rule articulated (methodology v1.2). 4-day sequential backfill complete; series is current.
item.description: Compassion Benchmark daily briefing for 2026-05-11.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 10: Day-2 ceasefire confirms bad-faith inverted-signal methodology — Russia's cumulative breach record spans 5 events. UNAIDS 25.6% HIV visit drop strengthens Senegal band crossing. Pacific boundary cluster identified: Timor-Leste, Kiribati, Marshall Islands all at 39.1 (0.9 below Functional floor).
item.description: Compassion Benchmark daily briefing for 2026-05-10.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 9: Hungary — HOLD RELEASED. Peter Magyar sworn in May 9. First-day enacted actions: EU flag flown at Parliament (SYS/BND); TV2 news director and pro-Orbán presenters dismissed (ACC/SYS); Sulyok dismissal demanded with May 31 hard deadline (ACC); €17B frozen EU funds named top priority with 27 EU rule-of-law supermilestones agreed pre-inauguration (ACC/BND/INT); inaugural address: 'system change / service not rule' framing. Five dimensions credited +0.5 each. Proposed 37.5 (Developing). First positive EU-cluster signal this rotation. Largest single-cycle positive delta in countries index this rotation.
item.description: Hungary — HOLD RELEASED. Peter Magyar sworn in May 9. First-day enacted actions: EU flag flown at Parliament (SYS/BND); TV2 news director and pro-Orbán presenters dismissed (ACC/SYS); Sulyok dismissal demanded with May 31 hard deadline (ACC); €17B frozen EU funds named top priority with 27 EU rule-o…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 8: Math-hygiene cluster extends to ai-labs index. India-Pakistan anniversary confirms structural entrenchment. Zero band changes. Zero floor designations. Myanmar hospital-targeting conduct documented.
item.description: Compassion Benchmark daily briefing for 2026-05-08.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 7: Character.AI — NEW FLOOR DESIGNATION. Consumer AI harm-by-design: Pennsylvania AG May 5 2026 first-of-its-kind enforcement action over chatbot medical impersonation; chatbot 'Emilie' posed as licensed psychiatrist with fake license number, offered clinical assessment to depressed user. January 2026 settlement of five wrongful death lawsuits including two minor children who died by suicide. 20M+ monthly active users; no model card, no system card, no third-party safety audit; reactive-only remediation pattern. All 8 dimensions to floor (1). Composite 23.8 → 0.
item.description: A 19-entity cycle produced four material downgrade proposals and the second consecutive floor designation in as many days. Character.AI (composite 23.8 → 0, developing → critical/floor) is the first floor entity in the cluster whose designation rests on consumer-product harm-by-design: chatbots impe…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 6: Ghost Robotics first agent baseline — NEW FLOOR DESIGNATION proposed. Robotics-labs index first floor entity. Cedra AI April 13 2026 ethics analysis documents explicit refusal to restrict military payload use ('we don't know what they do with them'); refusal to sign 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signed by 6 peer firms; sniper-rifle-equipped quadruped deployment to US-Mexico border. No model card, system card, safety policy, or third-party red-teaming for any product. All 8 dimensions to floor (1). Composite 9.4 → 0.
item.description: A 14-entity priority cycle produced three material downgrade proposals and the first floor designation in the robotics-labs index. Ghost Robotics (composite 9.4 → 0) was designated floor on its first baseline, establishing a precedent: explicit policy refusal to restrict military payload use — witho…
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 5: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — May 5
item.description: Math-hygiene cluster grew from 3 to 8 entities in one cycle, now spanning all five bands — bidirectional discrepancies rule out a single formula error; bulk data-team audit overdue.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark

item.title: May 4: Compassion Benchmark Daily Briefing — May 4
item.description: Alphabet/Google: 600+ employees revolt against Pentagon classified-AI deal on the same day Fortune documents the governance regression from Project Maven — assessed composite moves to 37.4 (-3.2), sub-threshold but the most evidentially significant finding of the cycle.
item.dc:creator: Compassion Benchmark
